config.title: Alte Nationalgalerie Berlin FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers about tickets, highlights, opening hours and whether the Old National Gallery is worth visiting.

items.q: What is the Alte Nationalgalerie in Berlin?
items.a: **The Alte Nationalgalerie is Museum Island’s 19th-century art museum.** It shows Romanticism, Realism, Impressionism, sculpture and the bridge toward modern art.

items.q: Why does it look like a Greek temple?
items.a: The temple form was intentional. Friedrich Wilhelm IV wanted a raised **sanctuary for art and science**, and Stüler developed the temple-like design.
Start with **Caspar David Friedrich**, especially the Friedrich room, then look for Adolph Menzel, Schinkel, Schadow’s Princess Group and the Impressionist rooms.

items.q: How much is the Alte Nationalgalerie ticket?
items.a: As of the checked 2026 source, the museum ticket for Alte Nationalgalerie plus Cassirer is **EUR 16**, reduced **EUR 8**; Museum Island is **EUR 24**, reduced **EUR 12**.

items.q: Is the Alte Nationalgalerie open on Mondays?
items.a: No. The current public listing shows **Monday closed** and **Tuesday to Sunday, 10:00 to 18:00**. Always re-check before visiting.

items.q: Is it worth visiting if I only have one Museum Island stop?
items.a: Choose it if you love paintings, Romantic landscapes or Impressionism. If you want ancient Egypt or archaeology first, the Neues Museum or Altes Museum may fit better.
